div.p: Concurrent HTTP Server — All Components & Interfaces
atomic_store(&shutdown_flag, 1) async-signal-safe only NO malloc / mutex / printf

memset(pool, 0) pthread_mutex_init(&pool→lock) pthread_cond_init(&pool→not_empty) pthread_cond_init(&pool→not_full) for i=0..N-1: pthread_create(&pool→threads[i], worker_thread, pool) on any failure: rollback all returns 0 or -1
lock → pool→shutdown=1 pthread_cond_broadcast(not_empty) unlock for i=0..N-1: pthread_join(threads[i]) pthread_mutex_destroy / cond_destroy
lock if count ≥ 1024: unlock → return -1 queue[tail] = {client_fd, ip, port} tail = (tail+1) % 1024 count++ pthread_cond_signal(not_empty) unlock → return 0
+0x00 client_fd int (4 B) +0x04 client_ip char[16] (16 B) +0x14 client_port int (4 B) value-copied into/out of queue

ol.li: close(client_fd) at end of handle_client_keep_alive() → CLOSED Exactly one open, exactly one close. serve_file() does NOT close it.

div.p: HTTP/1.1 503 Service Unavailable Content-Length: 0 Connection: close Retry-After: 1 send() + close(client_fd)

while (!atomic_load(&shutdown_flag)) client_fd = accept(server_fd, ...) errno==EINTR → continue errno==EMFILE/ENFILE → usleep(100ms) + continue thread_pool_enqueue(&g_pool, client_fd, ip, port) on -1: send HTTP 503 + close(client_fd)
close(server_fd) thread_pool_shutdown(&g_pool) access_log_close(&g_log) return EXIT_SUCCESS
head — dequeue index tail — enqueue index count — current occupancy capacity = 1024 (power-of-2) item size = 24 bytes each total = 24 576 bytes
lock pthread_mutex_t (40 B) not_empty pthread_cond_t (48 B) not_full pthread_cond_t (48 B) shutdown int (0=run / 1=drain+exit)
thread_count = N (default 16) each handle used in pthread_join() during thread_pool_shutdown()
item = queue[head] (24-byte copy) head = (head+1) % 1024 count-- pthread_cond_signal(not_full) pthread_mutex_unlock ← RELEASE

stats_connection_opened(&g_stats) handle_client_keep_alive( item.client_fd, item.client_ip, item.client_port, g_doc_root) ← closes client_fd before returning stats_connection_closed(&g_stats) → reacquire lock, loop
atomic_load(shutdown_flag)? requests ≥ MAX_REQUESTS(100)? → break if either true
select(fd, timeout=30s) on timeout → return -1 → break on EINTR → check shutdown, retry recv() loop until \r\n\r\n found returns >0 / 0(EOF) / -1(error)
HTTP_PARSE_BAD_REQUEST → 400 + break HTTP_PARSE_URI_TOO_LONG → 414 + break HTTP_PARSE_NOT_IMPL → 501 + break HTTP_PARSE_OK → continue
URL decode → realpath → prefix check MIME detect → stat → 304 check open → send headers → stream body Handles 200/304/403/404/500 does NOT close client_fd
Connection: close → 0 Connection: keep-alive → 1 HTTP/1.1 default → 1 HTTP/1.0 default → 0
lock pthread_mutex_t (40 B) active_connections int total_connections long long total_requests long long total_bytes_sent long long — cache lines 0–1 (72 B total)
lock pthread_mutex_t file FILE * (fopen append) format: IP:PORT - - [date] "METHOD PATH" STATUS BYTES fflush() inside lock on every write
Set once in main() via realpath() Read-only after thread creation No lock needed (write-once/read-many) pthread_create() acts as memory barrier
